lines.en_lit: raises an objection to receiving, carrying, or sprinkling a non-priest, a mourner a drunk and a blemished disqualified. And likewise sitting, and likewise left disqualified. a conclusive refutation,

But wasn’t Rav Sheshet one who objected As Rav Sheshet said to the interpreter of Rav Ḥisda: Raise the dilemma before Rav Ḥisda: What carrying by a non-priest? He said to him: valid, and a verse supports me: “And they slaughtered the Paschal offering and the priests sprinkled with their hand, and the Levites flayed”
And Rav Sheshet objected to receiving, carrying, or sprinkling a non-priest, a mourner a drunk and a blemished disqualified.
After heard he objected But didn’t Rav Ḥisda cite a verse that they acted like benches

Rav Pappa raised a dilemma: What another scooped and placed into the hands we require: “His full hands,” and that is Or perhaps we require “And he shall take…and he shall bring” and that is not Let it stand
Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi raised a dilemma: scooped and died, what that another enter with his handful? Rabbi Ḥanina said Come and see the question of the earlier
Is that to say that Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi older But didn’t Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi say: Rabbi Ḥanina permitted me to drink cress juice on Shabbat
to drink obvious As we learned All food may be eaten by people for medicinal drink all drinks

Rather, Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi’s to grind and drink cress on Shabbat? What are the circumstances? If where there is danger it is permitted; and if where there is no danger, prohibited Actually, where there is danger, and this is the dilemma that he raised before him: Does heal, that to violate Shabbat for it, or not heal, and not violate Shabbat for it?
And what is different Rabbi Ḥanina? because he is an expert in medicines, as Rabbi Ḥanina said: No man ever consulted me about a wound white mule and recovered.
But we see that do survive. say: and survives, But we see that does heal. We say when are red and the tops of their legs are white.
In any event, learn from this that Rabbi Ḥanina older Rather, this is he said, Their question is like the question of the early ones.
And did Rabbi Ḥanina say that But didn’t Rabbi Ḥanina say: with a bull” with a bull and not with the blood of bull?
Rabbi Ḥanina said: scooped the incense before the slaughtering of the bull, he did nothing,
this is said: From raised this dilemma, by inference that he holds “with a bull” even with the blood of a bull. And according to that which maintains, his question is like the question of the earlier
What was about Rav Pappa said: If scoops again scoops another enter with the handful as scooping fulfilled. if not scoop and again scoop, let the dilemma be raised.

Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, said to Rav Pappa: On the contrary, if scoops and again scoops, another not enter with his handful, it is impossible will be neither less nor more But if he does not scoop and again scoop, let the dilemma be raised,
As a dilemma was raised before scoop and again scoop a handful or not Come hear This was the measure What, is it not just as its measure on the outside, so is its measure on the inside,
No, Perhaps that if he wants to measure amount he may measure Alternatively, that neither less nor more
Come hear
